Breast cancer: presentation, investigation and management.
The Role of Progesterone Receptors in Breast Cancer.
Breast cancer in young women: an overview.

open_targets.name: breast cancer
open_targets.description: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm involving the breast. The vast majority of cases are carcinomas arising from the breast parenchyma or the nipple. Malignant breast neoplasms occur more frequently in females than in males.

open_targets.name: breast carcinoma
open_targets.description: A carcinoma that arises from epithelial cells of the breast

open_targets.name: Hereditary breast and ovarian cancer syndrome
open_targets.description: An autosomal dominant inherited syndrome caused by mutations in the BRCA1 or BRCA2 genes. Patients are at high risk of developing breast cancer, particularly before the age of fifty, high risk of developing a second primary breast cancer, and high risk of developing both breast and ovarian cancer.

open_targets.name: hereditary breast ovarian cancer syndrome
open_targets.description: An autosomal dominant inherited syndrome caused by mutations in the BRCA1 or BRCA2 genes. Patients are at high risk of developing breast cancer, particularly before the age of fifty, high risk of developing a second primary breast cancer, and high risk of developing both breast and ovarian cancer.

open_targets.name: Hereditary breast cancer
open_targets.description: Breast carcinoma that has developed in relatives of patients with history of breast carcinoma.

hypotheses.title: Modulating estrogen metabolism via gut microbiota to reduce circulating estrogen levels in premenopausal women.
hypotheses.mechanism: Inhibition of bacterial β‑glucuronidase reduces deconjugation of circulating estrogen conjugates, lowering bioavailable estrogen that drives ER‑positive tumor growth.
hypotheses.rationale: Elevated estrogen exposure is a key driver of ER‑positive breast cancer; recent studies show gut microbes influence estrogen recycling, and dietary patterns that shift microbial composition alter β‑glucuronidase activity.
hypotheses.next_test: Pilot cohort study measuring fecal β‑glucuronidase activity and serum estrogen levels before and after a high‑fiber, low‑fat diet intervention.

hypotheses.title: Selective targeting of progesterone receptor signaling with biased agonists to induce differentiation of PR‑positive tumor cells.
hypotheses.mechanism: Biased PR agonists activate transcriptional programs that up‑regulate cell‑cycle inhibitors (e.g., p21) while avoiding proliferative MAPK signaling.
hypotheses.rationale: PR positivity is associated with distinct tumor biology; laboratory work demonstrates that certain PR ligands can modulate gene expression without enhancing proliferation.
hypotheses.next_test: Ex vivo drug sensitivity screen of PR‑positive breast cancer cell lines with a panel of PR‑biased agonists, measuring p21 induction and proliferation rates.

hypotheses.title: Low‑dose aspirin may suppress inflammation‑driven tumorigenesis in BRCA mutation carriers by dampening COX‑2–NF‑κB signaling.
hypotheses.mechanism: Aspirin inhibition of COX‑2 reduces prostaglandin E2 production, leading to decreased NF‑κB activation and lower expression of pro‑inflammatory cytokines that promote tumor initiation.
hypotheses.rationale: Chronic inflammation contributes to breast cancer risk, especially in high‑risk genetic backgrounds; epidemiological data suggest aspirin use lowers cancer incidence.
hypotheses.next_test: Phase II chemoprevention trial enrolling BRCA1/2 carriers to receive low‑dose aspirin for 12 months, with endpoints of inflammatory biomarker levels and early‑stage lesion detection.

hypotheses.title: Time‑restricted feeding aligns circadian expression of DNA repair genes, enhancing repair capacity during vulnerable DNA replication phases.
hypotheses.mechanism: Restricting food intake to the active phase coincides with peak transcription of nucleotide excision repair genes, potentially improving repair of chemotherapy‑induced DNA damage.
hypotheses.rationale: Circadian dysregulation is linked to increased cancer risk; animal studies show feeding windows modulate DNA repair enzyme levels.
hypotheses.next_test: Measure circadian expression of XPA and DDB2 in peripheral blood mononuclear cells of breast cancer survivors following an 8‑hour time‑restricted feeding protocol.

hypotheses.title: Soy isoflavones act as selective estrogen receptor modulators that inhibit proliferation of HER2‑negative, ER‑low breast cancer cells.
hypotheses.mechanism: Isoflavones bind ER with partial antagonism, reducing transcription of proliferation‑associated genes while preserving anti‑oxidant activity.
hypotheses.rationale: Population studies suggest moderate soy consumption is not harmful and may be protective; laboratory data show SERM activity of isoflavones on ER‑negative cells.
hypotheses.next_test: Pharmacodynamic study assessing ER phosphorylation status and proliferation markers in patients receiving a standardized soy isoflavone supplement for 8 weeks.

hypotheses.title: Dietary omega‑3 fatty acids may down‑regulate FGFR1 signaling, slowing tumor growth in FGFR1‑amplified breast cancer subtypes.
hypotheses.mechanism: Omega‑3s bind to lipid rafts and inhibit FGFR1 autophosphorylation, leading to reduced downstream PI3K‑AKT signaling.
hypotheses.rationale: FGFR1 amplification is observed in a subset of aggressive breast cancers; preclinical work indicates that fatty acid metabolism can affect receptor activity.
hypotheses.next_test: Randomized dietary intervention trial assigning patients with FGFR1‑amplified tumors to an omega‑3 rich diet versus control, with tumor biomarker changes as primary outcome.

hypotheses.title: Targeting tumor‑derived lactate acidosis with systemic sodium bicarbonate therapy to normalize pH and impair metastatic niche formation.
hypotheses.mechanism: Elevated lactate acidifies the microenvironment, promoting invasion and immune evasion; alkalinization reduces acidity, restoring antitumor immune cell function.
hypotheses.rationale: Acidic tumor pH is a recognized hallmark of aggressive disease; early clinical reports show sodium bicarbonate modulates pH and improves response to chemotherapy.
hypotheses.next_test: Preclinical mouse model study assessing metastatic burden after supplementation with sodium bicarbonate in combination with anti‑metastatic monoclonal antibody.

hypotheses.title: Epigenetic priming with dietary methyl donors (e.g., folate, betaine) can re‑activate silenced tumor suppressor pathways in estrogen receptor–negative cancers.
hypotheses.mechanism: Increased methyl‑group donation enhances DNA methylation at promoters of differentiation agents, restoring expression of tumor suppressor genes like BRCA1‑associated protein 1 (BARD1).
hypotheses.rationale: Epigenetic alterations drive ER‑negative tumorigenesis; nutrient‑based modulation of methylation offers a reversible therapeutic avenue.
hypotheses.next_test: Phase I trial measuring changes in promoter methylation of BARD1 and RASSF1A in peripheral blood DNA of patients receiving a folate‑enhanced diet for 6 months.

hypotheses.title: Gut‑derived short‑chain fatty acids may potentiate immune checkpoint blockade by enhancing T‑cell infiltration into breast tumors.
hypotheses.mechanism: SCFAs such as butyrate inhibit histone deacetylases in infiltrating T cells, increasing expression of CCR7 and promoting migration toward tumor chemokines.
hypotheses.rationale: Microbiome metabolites influence anti‑PD‑1 therapy efficacy; breast cancer models show that SCFA supplementation improves response rates.
hypotheses.next_test: Combination trial of oral butyrate sodium with pembrolizumab in metastatic triple‑negative breast cancer, measuring immune cell infiltrate by multiplex IHC.

hypotheses.title: Physical activity‑induced myokine release (irisin) may enhance DNA damage response in breast cancer cells through up‑regulation of PARP1.
hypotheses.mechanism: Exercise‑derived irisin activates AMPK signaling, leading to increased PARP1 transcription and more efficient repair of double‑strand breaks after chemotherapy.
hypotheses.rationale: Exercise improves outcomes in breast cancer survivors; recent studies link irisin to DNA repair pathways, suggesting a Molecular bridge.
hypotheses.next_test: Exercise intervention study measuring circulating irisin levels and PARP1 expression in tumor biopsies before and after a 12‑week moderate‑intensity regimen.
